content:
# Deuteronomy 17 — Disagreement Log
**SOPHIA Arbiter** | Sources: EMET (E), LOGOS (L), PNEUMA (P)

---

## v.1 — "any evil thing" vs. "any defect whatever" vs. "serious flaw"

- **E:** "any evil thing" (*kol davar ra'*)
- **L:** "any defect whatever"
- **P:** "any defect or serious flaw"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "any defect whatever" (L). E's "evil thing" is literal but misleadingly moral in English; P's "serious flaw" adds a qualifier absent in the Hebrew.

---

## v.2 — "in the eyes of the LORD" vs. "in the sight of the LORD"

- **E:** "what is evil in the eyes of the LORD"
- **L:** "what is evil in the sight of the LORD"
- **P:** "has done evil in the sight of the LORD"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "in the eyes of the LORD" (E) consistently throughout Deuteronomy as the more literal *be-'eyne YHWH*.

---

## v.3 — "host of heaven" vs. "stars of the sky"

- **E:** "the host of heaven"
- **L:** "the host of heaven"
- **P:** "the stars of the sky"
- **SOPHIA:** Retains "the host of heaven" (E/L) — *tseva ha-shamayim* is a technical term for celestial beings/bodies in a cultic context; "stars of the sky" (P) is too narrow.

---

## v.4 — "inquire thoroughly" vs. "inquire diligently" vs. "investigate thoroughly"

- **E:** "you inquire thoroughly"
- **L:** "you shall inquire diligently"
- **P:** "investigate it thoroughly"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "you inquire diligently" (L).

---

## v.4 — "this abomination was done in Israel" vs. "in your midst"

- **E:** "this abomination was done in Yisra'el" (uses Hebrew name)
- **L:** "such an abomination has been done in Israel"
- **P:** "if this detestable thing has really happened in Israel"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "this abomination was done in Israel" (L). E's "Yisra'el" form is noted; SOPHIA uses "Israel" throughout as the English name form per project convention.

---

## v.5 — "to your gates" vs. "to your town gate"

- **E:** "you shall bring out that man or that woman…to your gates"
- **L:** "you shall bring out to your gates"
- **P:** "take that man or woman who has done this evil thing to your town gate"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "to your gates" (E/L). P's singular "town gate" is interpretively clarifying but reduces the MT's plural.

---

## v.7 — "purge the evil" vs. "burn out the evil"

- **E:** "you shall burn out the evil from your midst" (*u-bi'arta ha-ra'*)
- **L:** "So you shall purge the evil from your midst"
- **P:** "You must purge the evil from among you"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "you shall burn out the evil from your midst" (E) — the Deuteronomic refrain *bi'er* means to burn out/consume, and "burn out" preserves the vivid Hebrew idiom more closely than "purge."

---

## v.8 — triadic distinctions rendering

- **E:** "between blood and blood, between plea and plea, between stroke and stroke"
- **L:** "between one kind of bloodshed and another, between one kind of legal right and another, or between one kind of assault and another"
- **P:** "matters involving bloodshed, lawsuits, or assaults" (collapses the triadic pairs)
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts a blend: "between one kind of bloodshed and another, between one kind of legal claim and another, between one kind of assault and another." E's word-for-word is opaque; P collapses the structure; L's "legal right" is slightly misleading.

---

## v.9 — "declare to you the word of the judgment" vs. "declare to you the decision of the case"

- **E:** "they shall declare to you the word of the judgment"
- **L:** "they shall declare to you the decision of the case"
- **P:** "they will give you the verdict"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "they shall declare to you the decision of the case" (L). P's "verdict" is too narrow; E's "word of the judgment" is wooden.

---

## v.11 — torah gloss retained vs. not

- **E:** "According to the instruction [torah] that they instruct you"
- **L:** "According to the instruction that they give you"
- **P:** "Act in accordance with whatever ruling they give you"
- **SOPHIA:** Retains bracketed gloss [instruction] on *torah* per project convention. P's "ruling" is acceptable but generic.

---

## v.12 — "acts presumptuously" vs. "shows contempt"

- **E:** "the man who acts presumptuously" (*zadon*)
- **L:** "the man who acts presumptuously"
- **P:** "Anyone who shows contempt for the judge"
- **SOPHIA:** Retains "acts presumptuously" (E/L) for *zadon* — presumptuousness (deliberate defiance) is the specific concept; "contempt" (P) partially overlaps but shifts the register.

---

## v.15 — "you shall indeed set" vs. "you shall surely set"

- **E:** "you shall surely set over you a king" (*som tasim*)
- **L:** "you shall indeed set a king over you"
- **P:** "be sure to appoint over yourself the king"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "you shall indeed set over yourself a king" (L) — the cognate construction *som tasim* expresses certainty/permission, which "indeed" captures well.

---

## v.17 — "multiply wives" vs. "acquire many wives" vs. "take many wives"

- **E:** "he must not multiply wives for himself" (*lo yarbeh-lo nashim*)
- **L:** "he shall not acquire many wives for himself"
- **P:** "He must not take many wives for himself"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "he must not multiply wives for himself" (E) — the triple *lo yarbeh* structure of vv.16–17 uses the same verb in all three prohibitions; maintaining "multiply" preserves the rhetorical parallelism.

---

## v.18 — mishneh ha-torah: gloss and rendering

- **E:** "a copy of this instruction [mishneh ha-torah ha-zot]"
- **L:** "a copy of this instruction"
- **P:** "a copy of this law"
- **SOPHIA:** Retains Hebrew term with gloss: "a copy of this instruction [mishneh ha-torah ha-zot]." P's "law" is a less specific rendering of *torah* in this context.

---

## v.20 — "prolong [days]" vs. "continue long" vs. "reign for a long time"

- **E:** "so that he may prolong [days] in his kingdom"
- **L:** "so that he may continue long in his kingdom"
- **P:** "Then he and his descendants will reign for a long time"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "prolong his days in his kingdom" (E with supplied "his days"). P omits the "days" idiom and shifts to "descendants" for "sons" (*banim*).

---

## v.20 — "children" vs. "sons"

- **E:** "he and his sons"
- **L:** "he and his children"
- **P:** "he and his descendants"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "he and his children" (L) as natural English for *banim* in this dynastic context.

---

*Total disagreements logged: 14*
